name: Salmon with 2 Purees
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the onions and roughly dice them. Set aside.
2. Remove and discard celery root tops. Using a paring knife, peel and discard the thick black outer layer. Cut the celery root into pieces large enough to fit in the food processor hopper. Shred the celery root with the shredding blade. Combine onion and celery root in a heavy pot. Cover and place over low heat. Cook for 20 minutes, stirring frequently. Remove the cover and cook, stirring another 10 minutes or so until the mixture is dry. Keep warm while preparing the rest of the recipe.
3. Place mushrooms, shallots, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a food processor and puree until smooth. Scrape this mixture into a medium saucepan over medium heat and cook, uncovered, stirring until the moisture has evaporated and the mixture is dry. Remove from the heat and hold in a hot oven until salmon is done.
4. Preheat the broiler. Butter a 9-inch baking dish.
5. While cooking the mushroom mixture, combine white wine, garlic, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in another medium saucepan over medium heat. Cook, uncovered, until reduced by half. Add fish stock and reduce again until the mixture starts to thicken. Add cream and reduce until the sauce coats the back of a spoon. Scrape into a blender, add parsley, and blend until smooth. Replace in saucepan.
6. Place salmon in the baking dish and coat the surface with melted butter. Sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon salt and place under broiler for 5 minutes. Replace parsley cream over medium heat on top of the stove and whisk in unsalted butter. To serve, place dollops of celery root and mushroom puree on a warm plate. Lay a piece of salmon on top between the two and spoon some sauce over the top.
